chain.thought: Explanation: The candidate who is both elected (listed as incumbent) and unelected (listed as a party candidate) appears when the same name is in both the incumbent and party columns. In all such cases in the table, the 2008 status is “re-election.”

judge_thought: Explanation: The phrase "both elected and unelected" refers to an incumbent who is currently elected (listed under "incumbent") and also appears as a candidate (listed under a party column) for the 2008 election. In the table, incumbents like Diana DeGette, John Salazar, Marilyn Musgrave, Doug Lamborn, and Ed Perlmutter appear both as incumbents and as party nominees. The 2008 status for such candidates is "re-election," as shown for their districts.
